title: Workplace Safety & Health Coordinator (WSHC) cum Lifting Coordinator
description:
Roles & Responsibilities

The WSHC cum Lifting Coordinator shall support site management in ensuring compliance with the Workplace Safety and Health Act, its subsidiary legislation, and all applicable Codes of Practice, Approved Codes, and MOM requirements, particularly for lifting operations.

  1. Assist the Project Manager / WSH Officer in implementing, monitoring, and maintaining the Workplace Safety & Health Management System in accordance with statutory and project requirements.

  2. Plan, coordinate, and oversee lifting operations on site in compliance with the Workplace Safety and Health (Operation of Cranes) Regulations, approved lifting plans, risk assessments, and safe work procedures.

  3. Prepare, review, and update Risk Assessments (RA), Safe Work Procedures (SWP), method statements, and lifting plans for construction and lifting-related works.

  4. Conduct regular site safety inspections and monitor lifting operations to identify unsafe acts, unsafe conditions, and non-compliance, and ensure timely implementation of corrective and preventive actions.

  5. Ensure all cranes, lifting machines, lifting gears, and accessories are properly certified, inspected, tested, and maintained in accordance with statutory requirements before use.

  6. Coordinate and liaise with lifting supervisors, crane operators, riggers, signalmen, subcontractors, and site personnel to ensure lifting works are carried out safely and in accordance with approved plans.

  7. Verify that all personnel involved in lifting operations and safety-critical activities are competent, trained, authorised, and hold valid certificates as required under the regulations.

  8. Conduct toolbox meetings, safety briefings, safety inductions, and pre-lift briefings to communicate hazards, control measures, and safe work practices.

  9. Ensure proper implementation of barricades, exclusion zones, warning signage, traffic control measures, and permit-to-work systems for lifting and other high-risk activities.

  10. Assist in the investigation and reporting of accidents, dangerous occurrences, and near misses, including lifting-related incidents, and recommend corrective and preventive measures.

  11. Maintain accurate and up-to-date WSH and lifting records, including inspection checklists, permits, lifting plans, equipment certificates, and statutory registers.

  12. Support and coordinate MOM inspections, audits, and client safety inspections, and ensure timely closure of findings and non-compliances.

  13. Promote a positive safety culture on site and exercise the authority to stop unsafe work where there is an imminent risk to safety or health.

  14. Report safety, health, and lifting-related matters to site management promptly and accurately.

  15. Perform any other duties related to workplace safety and lifting coordination as assigned by management.

Job Requirements

  1. Possess a Certificate or Advanced Certificate in Workplace Safety & Health (WSH) Level B and/or other relevant safety certifications recognised by MOM.

  2. Minimum 3 years of relevant working experience in high-rise building construction projects.

  3. Good interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to liaise effectively with workers, supervisors, subcontractors, consultants, and authorities.

  4. Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced construction environment.

  5. Willingness to work rotating shifts, including night shifts, as required by project operations.

  6. Able to commit to a 6-day work week.

postedCompany.description:
China Communications Construction Company Ltd. is a world leading super large infrastructure service provider, ranked 93th among Fortune Global 500 in 2019. As China's largest international contractor, we ranked the third among ENR’s Top250 International Contractors and the first among Chinese enterprises listed for 13 consecutive years.


We are mainly engaged in infrastructure investment, construction and operation, equipment manufacturing, real estate and urban development, etc., and offer clients package solutions and integrated services such as investment, financing, consultation, planning, design, construction, management and operation.
postedCompany.name: CHINA COMMUNICATIONS CONSTRUCTION COMPANY LIMITED (Singapore Branch)
